title: Organize Digital Files
summary: Create folders and name files so digital documents are easy to find later.
learnerPromise: You will be able to set up a simple folder system on your computer or phone and sort your files so you can find them quickly.
whyItMatters: Digital clutter can be just as stressful as physical clutter. A clear folder structure and consistent naming make it faster to find documents, photos, and downloads when you need them.
realLifeUses: Create folders for school subjects so each class has its own place
Organize photos by month or event so they are easier to browse
Sort downloads into categories so your downloads folder stops overflowing
Set up a folder for important documents like IDs, medical forms, and receipts
materialsNeeded: Computer, tablet, or phone with a file manager app
result: A folder system with sorted files and a clear structure you can keep using.
youWillLearn: How to create top-level folders for broad categories
How to move files into the correct folders
How to delete or archive files you no longer need
How to name folders in a way that makes sense for your needs
miniChallenge: Create 3 to 5 folders for your most common file types and move at least 10 files into them.
steps: Open your files app or file explorer and look at where your files currently live.
Create 3 to 5 main folders for broad categories like School, Work, Photos, and Personal.
Go through your files one by one and move each into the most fitting folder.
Delete or move to a separate archive folder anything you no longer need.
Check that you can find a file in under 10 seconds using your new system.
completionCriteria: You have created 3 to 5 clearly named folders
At least 10 files have been moved into appropriate folders
You can locate any common file quickly without searching
commonProblems: Creating too many folders so the system becomes hard to navigate
Naming folders with vague labels like 'Stuff' that do not mean anything later
Being afraid to delete files and ending up with a bloated archive
tips: Keep your folder structure flat, 2 levels deep is usually enough
Use broad category names for top folders and specific names for subfolders
Set a monthly reminder to spend 10 minutes tidying your files
